items.description: This painting depicts a young woman lost in reverie after reading the letters of the ill-fated medieval lovers Heloise and Abelard. The objects on the table beside her—a letter, a sheet of music, and a book of erotic poetry—hint at a life of leisure and a susceptibility to love. In this early picture, Auguste Bernard drew upon history paintings by Peter Paul Rubens and Charles Le Brun, as well as Parisian traditions of genre painting and portraiture pioneered by Jean-Baptiste Greuze. Bernard worked in Paris in the early 1780s and studied in Italy for several years. Upon his return to Paris, he found his career frustrated by the French Revolution and the emergent fashion for the more austere Neoclassical style.
items.provenance: Louis Gabriel Marquis de Véri-Raionard (died 1785); his estate sale, Paris, December 12, 1785, lot 74; sold to Devouge for 371 livres [acc. to annotated copies of the catalogue in the Rijksbureau voor Kunsthistorische Documentatie, The Hague, and Getty Research Institute].  David David-Weill, Neuilly-sur-Seine, Paris; sold or consigned to Wildenstein, Paris, 1919; sold by Wildenstein, 1924 [acc. to Joseph Baillio’s letter to Martha Wolff, dated September 23, 1994, in curatorial file].  Albert John Kobler (died 1937), New York; by descent to his wife, Mignon Kobler (died 1942) [acc. to Martha Wolff’s telephone conversation with John Kobler, Albert’s son, May 30, 1995, and Chrisopher Apostle, Sotheby's who confirmed that the picture was consigned to Parke-Bernet by the Kobler estate; see his letter to Martha Wolff, dated October 26, 2004, in curatorial file]; sold Parke-Bernet, New York, April 22, 1948, lot 16 (ill.), as Jean Baptiste Greuze, L’Amoureux Desir; Nicholas de Koenigsberg, New York and Argentina [acc. to Parke-Bernet Galleries’ archive card].  Simon Dickinson Inc., New York, by 1994; sold to the Art Institute, 1994.
items.title_zh: 阅读埃洛伊丝和阿贝拉尔书信的女士
items.description_zh: 这幅画描绘了一位年轻女子在阅读不幸的中世纪恋人埃洛伊丝和阿贝拉尔的书信后陷入遐想。她身旁桌上的物品——一封信、一张乐谱和一本情色诗集——暗示着悠闲的生活和对爱情的易感性。在这幅早期作品中，奥古斯特·贝尔纳借鉴了彼得·保罗·鲁本斯和查尔斯·勒布伦的历史画，以及让-巴蒂斯特·格勒兹开创的巴黎风俗画和肖像画传统。贝尔纳于1780年代早期在巴黎工作，并在意大利学习了数年。返回巴黎后，他发现自己的职业生涯因法国大革命和新古典主义风格的兴起而受挫。
items.cardline_zh: 信笺间，中世纪的叹息落在她低垂的眼睑。
items.cardline: Medieval sighs fall on her lowered lids.

items.title: The Home of the Heron
items.description:
The landscape painter George Inness once explained, “The true purpose of the painter is simply to reproduce in other minds the impression which a scene has made upon him . . . to awaken an emotion.” Inness sought, particularly in his later years, to record not so much the appearance of nature as its poetry. To achieve this, he limited his subject matter to, in his words, “rivers, streams, the rippling brook, the hillside, the sky, the clouds.” For half a century, the artist captured these moisture-laden subjects in all seasons, during all hours of the day and night. First he made small, quick sketches in the field or wood, and then, in the seclusion of his studio, he used them to create the more than one thousand oils credited to him.
Inness completed The Home of the Heron near the end of his career, after he had finally achieved a degree of comfort and success. The painting is characteristic of his late work, with loosely rendered detail and dim objects that seem bathed in an almost incandescent glow. The picture’s blurred outlines, broad handling, and delicate, subtle tonalities, as well as the solitary presence of the heron, masterfully evoke nature’s stillness and mystery. With more than two dozen canvases by Inness, the Art Institute has one of the most comprehensive collections of the painter’s work.
items.provenance: Estate of the artist (sale, Fifth Avenue Galleries, New York, February 12-14, 1895, no. 69, as "The Sun's Last Reflection"); E.W. Bass, 1895; Emerson McMillin, New York, to 1911; Knoedler & Co., New York, 1911; Reinhardt Galleries, Chicago, 1911; sold to Edward B. Butler, Chicago, 1911; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1911.
items.description_zh:
风景画家乔治·英尼斯曾解释说：“画家的真正目的仅仅是在他人心中再现一个场景给他留下的印象……唤醒一种情感。”英尼斯，尤其是在晚年，试图记录的不是自然的外貌，而是其诗意。为此，他将题材限制在，用他的话来说，“河流、溪流、涟漪的小溪、山坡、天空、云彩”。半个世纪以来，这位艺术家捕捉了这些充满水汽的题材，涵盖所有季节、昼夜的各个时辰。他先在野外或树林中快速绘制小幅草图，然后在画室的僻静中，利用它们创作了归功于他的超过一千幅油画。
英尼斯在职业生涯接近尾声时完成了《苍鹭之家》，此时他终于获得了一定程度的舒适和成功。这幅画是他晚期作品的特点，细节松散，物体暗淡，仿佛沐浴在几乎炽热的光芒中。画面模糊的轮廓、宽大的处理手法和精致微妙的色调，以及苍鹭的孤独存在，巧妙地唤起了自然的宁静与神秘。凭借英尼斯的二十多幅画作，芝加哥艺术博物馆拥有这位画家最全面的收藏之一。
items.cardline_zh: 苍鹭归处，光影织成雾的帷幕。
items.cardline: Heron's home, light weaves a curtain of mist.

items.title: Yellow Dancers (In the Wings)
items.description: Edgar Degas first painted dancers as an independent subject in 1871. He was to devote almost half his output as an artist to this subject, observing countless performances and rehearsals at the Paris Opéra. Here he placed the viewer in the wings, as if among the elite Opéra subscribers who roamed and socialized backstage. Dance subjects allowed Degas to contemporize his lifelong interest in showing the human body in complex movement, shifting the scene from ancient history to modern Paris. He finished and signed the present canvas in time for the second Impressionist exhibition, in April 1876.
items.provenance: Shipped by the artist to the art dealer, Charles W. Deschamps, London, around May 15, 1876 [see letter of May 15, 1876 from Degas to Deschamps, Collection Frits Lugt, Institut Néerlandais, Paris, published in Reff 1968].  James Staats Forbes, London; sold to Goupil et Cie, Paris on July 25, 1891 [see Goupil-Boussod & Valadon Successeurs Ledgers, cited in John Rewald 1973]; sold to Potter Palmer (died 1902), Chicago on September 18, 1891 [see Goupil Ledgers citied above]; by descent in the Palmer family; given to the Art Institute, 1963.
items.description_zh: 埃德加·德加于1871年首次将舞者作为独立题材进行绘画。他将近一半的艺术作品都奉献给这一题材，在巴黎歌剧院观察了无数场演出和排练。在这里，他将观者置于侧台，仿佛置身于那些在后台游荡和社交的歌剧院精英订户之中。舞蹈题材使德加能够将他毕生对展示人体复杂运动的兴趣现代化，将场景从古代历史转移到现代巴黎。他在1876年4月第二届印象派展览前完成并签署了这幅画布。
items.cardline_zh: 侧幕间，芭蕾的颤动用柠檬黄写就。
items.cardline: Ballet's tremor written in lemon yellow.

items.title: Odalisque
items.description: A luminous nude woman reclines in a dimly lit interior filled with fruits, textiles, and other luxury goods. To European audiences, this collection of objects would have broadly evoked the Middle East. The painting’s setting and title suggest that she is a courtesan in a harem. For 19th-century European artists, depicting the nude female form was a fundamental component of academic training and a benchmark of artistic excellence. The imagined harem setting—a space historically used as women’s quarters and forbidden to men—provided narrative context for the nude and appealed to the sexual and colonialist fantasies of European audiences.
items.provenance: Vincent Astor, New York, by 1926; sold, sale of the Astor Residence, American Art Association, New York, April 21, 1926, lot 450, to George F. Harding, Jr. [buyer according to a Harding Museum record in the George F. Harding files, Art Institute, copy in curatorial file]; George F. Harding, Jr. (died 1929), Chicago; bequeathed to the George F. Harding Museum, Chicago; transferred to the Art Institute, 1983.
items.description_zh: 一位光彩照人的裸体女性斜倚在昏暗的内室中，周围摆满了水果、纺织品和其他奢侈品。对欧洲观众而言，这些物品会大致唤起对中东的联想。画作的场景和标题暗示她是一位后宫中的妓女。对于19世纪的欧洲艺术家来说，描绘女性裸体是学院训练的基本组成部分，也是艺术卓越的基准。想象的后宫场景——一个历史上用作女性居所且禁止男性进入的空间——为裸体提供了叙事背景，并迎合了欧洲观众的性幻想和殖民主义幻想。
items.cardline_zh: 幽室中，异域织物裹着慵懒的月。
items.cardline: In dim chamber, exotic cloths wrap a lazy moon.

items.title: A City Park
items.description: In the late 1880s, William Merritt Chase painted a series of works focused on the public parks of Brooklyn and Manhattan, including A City Park. Amid rapid growth in New York and other US cities, such green spaces offered respite as well as novel venues for social activity. Inspired by the works of French Impressionism, Chase experimented with dramatic spatial effects, open brushwork, and a high-keyed palette. The composition’s small scale suggests that the artist painted the canvas outdoors, opting for portable materials to work on-site in an urban setting.
items.provenance: William Merritt Chase, New York, from 1887 to 1891; Renaissance Gallery, Philadelphia, by 1950; Graham Gallery, New York City, 1950; Dr. John Jay Ireland, Chicago, 1956; bequeathed to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1968.
items.description_zh: 1880年代末，威廉·梅里特·蔡斯创作了一系列聚焦布鲁克林和曼哈顿公共公园的作品，包括《城市公园》。在纽约和其他美国城市快速发展的背景下，这样的绿色空间提供了喘息之机以及新颖的社交活动场所。受法国印象派作品的启发，蔡斯尝试了戏剧性的空间效果、开放的笔触和高调的色彩。构图的较小尺寸表明艺术家是在户外绘制这幅画布，选择了便携材料以便在城市环境中现场作画。
items.cardline_zh: 绿荫下，城市放缓了它匆忙的脚步。
items.cardline: Under green shade, city slows its hurried pace.

items.title: Terrace and Observation Deck at the Moulin de Blute-Fin, Montmartre
items.medium: Oil on canvas, mounted on pressboard
items.description: This painting dates from the winter of 1887, roughly a year after Vincent van Gogh arrived in Paris to join his brother, the art dealer Theo van Gogh. It is one of a group of landscapes featuring the Butte Montmartre, a short climb from the apartment on the rue Lepic where Vincent and Theo lived. Montmartre was dotted with reminders of its quickly receding rural past—abandoned quarries, kitchen gardens, and three surviving windmills, including the Moulin de Blute-Fin. The nonfunctional mill had become a tourist attraction, affording spectacular panoramic views over Paris from the observation tower erected beside it.
items.provenance: The artist’s sister in law, Johanna van Gogh-Bonger (died 1925), Amsterdam [this and the following according to Van Gogh-Bonger’s account book: "101/17 1/1924 'landschapje op Montmartre/lantaarn' 2500 [guilders] reproduced in Stolwijk and Veenenbos 2002, 136, 165]; sold to Mrs. Elizabeth Carstairs (née Stebbins, died 1949), London, Dec. 1923 for 2,500 guilders; sold through Ernest Brown and Philips (Leicester Galleries), London to M. Knoedler Art Gallery, Paris, Jan. 1924 for £300 [this and the following according to Knoedler Stock Book, no. 15822, Getty Research Institute, Los Angeles]; sold to Frederic Clay Bartlett (died 1953) and Helen Birch Bartlett (died 1925), New York, Aug. 1924, for £400; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1926.
items.title_zh: 蒙马特布吕特芬磨坊的露台和观景台
items.description_zh: 这幅画创作于1887年冬天，大约在文森特·梵高抵达巴黎与他的弟弟、画商提奥·梵高会合一年后。它是描绘蒙马特高地的一组风景画之一，从梵高兄弟居住的勒皮克街公寓出发只需短途攀登。蒙马特遍布着快速消逝的乡村过去的遗迹——废弃的采石场、菜园和三个幸存的磨坊，包括布吕特芬磨坊。这个已不再运作的磨坊已成为旅游景点，从它旁边建造的观景塔上可以欣赏到巴黎壮丽的全景。
items.cardline_zh: 蒙马特的高处，风车凝望着远去的田园。
items.cardline: Montmartre high, windmill gazes at fading fields.

items.title: Boats on the Beach at Étretat
items.description:
Forced indoors by inclement fall weather, Claude Monet painted Boats on the Beach at Étretat and The Departure of the Boats, Étretat while looking out the window of his room at the Hôtel Blanquet. The two form a pair that share a palette, subject, and vantage point. In one of his daily letters to his companion and future wife, Alice Hoschedé, dated November 24, 1885, Monet he described first Boats on the Beach and then Departure of the Boats: “In the afternoon, I worked in my room on my caloges [retired fishing boats covered with tarred planks and used for storage] in the rain, then I attempted to do, always through the window, a picture of the boats departing.”
Monet centered each composition on the boats, combining pastel blues, pinks, purples, and greens to render wet surfaces. The brightly colored hulls of beached crafts lend relative scale to the structures in Boats on the Beach, and the groups of figures at the water’s edge, composed of quick, gestural strokes, register human activity in Departure of the Boats.
items.provenance: Georges Bernaert [this and the following per Wildenstein 1996]. Georges Bernheim, Paris, c. 1907. Marczell de Nemes, Budapest, by 1911 [per Mayer 1911]; sold at the Marczell de Nemes Sale, Galerie Manzi-Joyant, Paris, June 18, 1913, lot 113, to Durand-Ruel, Paris, as an agent for an unnamed person, for 9,500 francs [per Galerie Manzi-Joyant 1913; see also Durand-Ruel Archives, annotated sales catalogue. The Durand-Ruel Archives also has a letter [in French translated here by G. Groom] from Durand-Ruel, Paris, to Durand-Ruel, New York, dated June 24, 1913, saying that they bought several important paintings, that were consigned but they would never have paid prices like that for these types of paintings” and that “ Durand-Ruel purchased 11 paintings at this sale, the names of the buyers are not recorded in our books”; see Paul-Louis Durand-Ruel and Flavie Durand-Ruel to the Art Institute of Chicago, Feb. 5, 2013, curatorial object file, Art Institute of Chicago. See also “Liste des prix et noms des acquéreurs de la deuxième vacation de la collection Marczell de Nemes,” Le Gil Blas, June 19, 1913, p. 2, which states that the asking price was 12,000 francs and that the painting was sold to M. Durand-Ruel for 9,500 francs]. Kleinberger Gallery, Paris, by Nov. 15, 1922 [per Rich 1938]. John Levy Galleries, Paris, by Nov. 15, 1922 [this and the following per purchase receipt on John Levy Galleries letterhead dated November 15, 1922, mentions the sale of this painting by John Levy Galleries for $2,295 as La Plage à Etretat to Mr. C. H. Worcester, copy in curatorial object file]; sold to Charles H. Worcester, Chicago, by Nov. 15, 1922, for $2,295; given by Charles H. and Mary F. S. Worcester to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1947 [the painting stayed in the Worcesters’ home until 1956, when Mr. Worcester died; see receipt of object 14829, on file in Museum Registration, Art Institute of Chicago. Mrs. Mary F. S. Worcester died in 1954].
items.title_zh: 埃特勒塔海滩上的船只
items.description_zh:
由于秋季天气恶劣被迫待在室内，克劳德·莫奈在布兰凯酒店房间的窗边创作了《埃特勒塔海滩上的船只》和《船只出发，埃特勒塔》。这两幅画构成一对，共享色调、题材和视角。在1885年11月24日写给伴侣兼未来妻子爱丽丝·奥什德的一封日常信件中，莫奈首先描述了《海滩上的船只》，然后是《船只出发》：“下午，我在房间里画我的‘卡洛日’（涂有柏油木板覆盖的退役渔船，用于储存），然后我尝试，始终透过窗户，画一幅船只出发的画。”
莫奈将每幅构图集中在船只上，结合柔和的蓝色、粉色、紫色和绿色来表现湿润的表面。在《海滩上的船只》中，搁浅船只色彩鲜艳的船体为结构提供了相对比例，而在《船只出发》中，水边由快速、示意性笔触构成的人物群组记录了人类活动。
items.cardline_zh: 窗框住船影，灰蓝的浪涌向永恒的岸。
items.cardline: Window frames boats, gray-blue waves lap eternal shores.

items.title: Boy on a Ram
items.artist: Francisco José de Goya y Lucientes (Spanish, 1746–1828)
items.description: As painter to the king of Spain, Francisco de Goya also designed tapestries for the royal residences. The artist made small oil sketches to work out the designs, followed by full-scale painted cartoons that served as guides for the weavers at the king’s tapestry works. This cartoon was produced for a tapestry in a series decorating the dining room in the Palace of El Pardo outside Madrid. The room’s large panels illustrate a traditional decorative subject, the four seasons, but Goya exercised his imagination in the smaller panels above the doors, producing scenes of children and animals like this one.
items.provenance: Real Fábrica de Tapices de Santa Bárbara, 1786/87 (painted as a cartoon for a series of tapestries executed there for Rey Carlos III for the palacio at El Pardo, outside Madrid); Livinio Stuyk y Vandergoten, by 1887 (director of the Real Fábrica de Tapices de Santa Bárbara); by descent to his son, Gabino Stuyk, Madrid, by 1900. Real Fábrica de Tapices, Madrid, by 1911 [this and the following according to a letter from March 19, 1991, from Melissa de Medeiros, Knoedler and Company, to Mary Kuzniar; copy in curatorial file]; sold to Knoedler and Company, London and New York, September 1911; sold to Charles Deering (died 1927), April 1912; by descent to his daughter, Mrs. Chauncey McCormick, Chicago; by descent to her son, Brooks McCormick, Chicago; given by Mr. and Mrs. Brooks McCormick to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1979.
items.description_zh: 作为西班牙国王的画家，弗朗西斯科·德·戈雅还为皇家住所设计挂毯。艺术家制作小幅油画草图来构思设计，然后绘制全尺寸的绘画草图，作为国王挂毯工厂织工的指南。这幅草图是为装饰马德里郊外埃尔帕尔多宫餐厅的一系列挂毯而制作的。房间的大幅面板展示了传统的装饰主题——四季，但戈雅在门上方的较小面板中发挥了他的想象力，创作了像这样的儿童和动物场景。
items.cardline_zh: 少年骑羊，嬉戏在壁毯的永恒春日。
items.cardline: Boy rides ram, playing in tapestry's eternal spring.

items.title: The Bathers
items.description:
Paul Cezanne set this idyllic view of a group of bathers in a frame of foliage. He rendered the ground, water, trees, and sky with patches of hatching that seem to register the energy he felt when painting. His primary concern, however, seems to have been striking a balance between the vigor of the bodies and the restfulness of the setting through individual poses and figure groupings—some invented, some likely derived from his other works depicting this subject.
Over the course of his career, Cezanne frequently revisited groups of bathers in nature. Through his multiple studies of the subject, he reconceived the classical motif of nudes outdoors in a modern style.
items.provenance: Ambroise Vollard (1866–1939), Paris. Jacques Zoubaloff (1876–1941), Paris, between 1918–1922; his sale, Galerie Georges Petit, Paris, June16–17, 1927, lot 113 (ill.). Jos. Hessel, Paris [according to Rewald 1996]. Paul Rosenberg and Co., Paris and New York, 1928 [New York 1928]. Chester H. Johnson Gallery, Chicago [Chicago 1933]. Mrs. Robert R. McCormick (Amy Irwin McCormick; 1872–1939), Chicago, by 1929 [see Chicago 1929 exhibition materials, Archives of American Art, copy in curatorial file]; By descent to her husband, Colonel Robert R. McCormick (1880–1955), Chicago; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1942.
items.description_zh:
保罗·塞尚将这一群浴者的田园风光置于树叶的框架中。他用成片的交叉排线渲染地面、水、树木和天空，似乎记录了他绘画时所感受到的能量。然而，他的主要关注点似乎是通过个体姿势和人物分组——有些是虚构的，有些可能源自他描绘这一主题的其他作品——在身体的活力与环境的宁静之间取得平衡。
在他的职业生涯中，塞尚经常重新审视自然中的浴者群体。通过他对这一主题的多重研究，他以现代风格重新构思了古典的户外裸体主题。
items.cardline_zh: 笔触织成叶隙，裸女与自然同呼吸。
items.cardline: Brushstrokes weave leaves, nudes breathe with nature.

items.title: Portrait of Marevna
items.description: Prior to achieving renown as a muralist, Diego Rivera lived in Paris amid Pablo Picasso’s circle, working in a Cubist manner. Portrait of Marevna depicts Rivera’s mistress, the Russian artist Maria Vorobieff-Stebelska. The couple’s relationship was tempestuous; Rivera later described Marevna as a “she-devil,” and he conveyed a sense of her ferocity in this portrait, particularly in the figure’s distinctive frown and squinting eye. The portrait exemplifies Rivera’s adoption of elements of Synthetic Cubism: he constructed images sequentially, building layer upon layer rather than deconstructing forms into fragmented planes. Rivera emphasized the geometric patterning of the composition through his use of textures, such as the rectangular brocade, which appears to be a piece of fabric or wallpaper but is in fact illusionistic paint, referencing Cubist collage techniques.
items.provenance: Alfred Stieglitz, New York (1864–1946) and Georgia O'Keeffe (1887–1986), New York and New Mexico, by 1949 [on loan to the Art Institute of Chicago from Apr. 21, 1949, incoming receipt, R9550; copy in curatorial object file]; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1957.
items.description_zh: 在成为著名的壁画家之前，迭戈·里维拉住在巴黎，身处巴勃罗·毕加索的圈子中，以立体主义风格创作。《马雷夫娜肖像》描绘了里维拉的情人、俄罗斯艺术家玛丽亚·沃罗比约夫-斯捷别尔斯卡。两人的关系充满激情；里维拉后来将马雷夫娜描述为“女魔头”，他在这幅肖像中传达了她的凶猛，尤其是人物独特的皱眉和眯眼。这幅肖像体现了里维拉对综合立体主义元素的采用：他按顺序构建图像，层层叠加，而不是将形式分解成碎片化的平面。里维拉通过使用纹理强调了构图的几何图案，例如矩形锦缎，看起来像是一块织物或墙纸，但实际上是错觉性的颜料，参考了立体主义的拼贴技巧。
items.cardline_zh: 棱角间，情人的火焰凝为沉静的棱镜。
items.cardline: In facets, lover's flame hardens to a prism.

items.title: Scenes from the Life of Saint John the Baptist
items.provenance: Possibly part of a suite of decorations belonging to Francesco di Bonacorso Pitti, Florence, from 1494 [based on arms in a related panel in National Gallery of Ireland, Dublin; see Lloyd 1993, p. 20].  Luigi Grassi, Florence, by 1924  [bill of sale dated March 18, 1924 in curatorial file]; sold to Martin A. Ryerson (died 1932), Chicago, 1924; on loan to the Art Institute, 1924; by descent to his wife Carrie Hutchinson Ryerson (1859–1937), Chicago, 1932 [Last Will and Testament of Martin A. Ryerson, Died August 11, 1932, copy in Institutional Archives, Art Institute of Chicago]; bequeathed to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1937.
items.cardline_zh: 金色底面，圣徒的脚步在传说中回响。
items.cardline: On gold ground, saint's steps echo in legend.

items.title: The Plate of Apples
items.description: The lozenge-shaped pattern of the wallpaper identifies the setting for this still life as the Paris apartment where Paul Cezanne and his family lived between 1875 and 1879. Here, the artist disrupted expectations for painting at the time by tipping the tabletop and plate forward toward the viewer and constructing a table with edges that don’t quite align. These disorienting details are complemented by the heavy, almost sculptural forms he created by using a pointed tool—possibly the end of his brush handle—to incise contours into semi-dry paint around the plate and some of the apples.
items.provenance: Eugène Murer, Auvers–sur–Oise and Paris, before Oct. 1887 [see Trublot 1887 and Rewald 1996]. Galerie Heinrich Thannhauser, Munich, by 1916 [Berlin 1916]. F. Schoen, Berlin [accordind to correspondence from P. Rosenberg and Co. to W. Brewster, Feb. 25, 1930, copy in curatorial file]. Paul Cassirer, Berlin, Sept. 29, 1925 [this and the following according to Rewald 1996; Paul Cassirer archive, PC18965]. J. K. Thannhauser, Lucerne, Switzerland, Feb. 2, 1926. Paul Rosenberg and Co., New York and Paris, by 1930 [this and the following according to Rosenberg to Brewster, Feb. 25, 1930]; sold to Walter S. Brewster, Chicago, (1872–1954), Feb. 15, 1930; Kate L. Brewster (Mrs. Walter S. Brewster; 1879–1947), Chicago; bequeathed to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1949.
items.description_zh: 墙纸的菱形图案确定了这幅静物画的场景是保罗·塞尚和他的家人于1875年至1879年间居住的巴黎公寓。在这里，艺术家通过将桌面和盘子向前倾斜朝向观者，并构建边缘不完全对齐的桌子，颠覆了当时绘画的预期。这些令人困惑的细节通过厚重、几乎雕塑般的形态得到补充，这些形态是他使用尖锐工具——可能是画笔柄的末端——在半干的颜料中围绕盘子和一些苹果刻出轮廓而创造的。
items.cardline_zh: 桌面倾斜，苹果的圆舞曲坠入永恒。
items.cardline: Table tilts, apple waltz falls into forever.

items.title: The Old Temple
items.description: The four large canvases by Hubert Robert in the Art Institute's collection were commissioned by wealthy ﬁnancier Jean-Joseph, Marquis de Laborde, to decorate a salon in his château at Méréville, France. Installed on all sides of the room, they would have suggested that the walls had dissolved, revealing fantasies of Classical architecture animated by scenes of everyday life. Robert was much sought after as a painter of architectural ruins, a genre that appealed to the late 18th-century taste for the antique. He cultivated his knowledge of antiquity over more than a decade of travel and study in Italy.
items.provenance: Commissioned with its pendants (1900.383, 1900.384, 1900.385) by Jean Joseph, marquis de Laborde (died 1794), in 1787 for the Château de Méréville (near Etampes); the château was sold by Mme de Laborde, 1819 [see Simone de Lassus, “Quelques Détails inédits sur Méréville,” Bulletin de la Société de l’histoire de l’art français, année 1976 (1978), p. 286 n. 1]; the château was owned successively by: M. Ters and Mme d’Espagnat (sold 1824); comte de Saint-Roman (sold 1866); duc de Sessa (sold 1868); M. and Mme Beleys (sold 1869); la Société Cail (sold 1874); M. Heddle (sold 1889); Adam Natanson (sold 1890); M. Hériot (sold 1896); Prudent Carpentier (sold 1897, at which time the contents of the house were dispersed and the paintings probably sold) [See Simone de Lassus 1978, cited above; a letter from Bernard Binvel to Susan Wise dated May 5, 1987 states, perhaps erroneously, that Hériot sold the paintings in July of 1896]. M. L. François; sold Galerie George Petit, Paris, June 13, 1900, no. 2, to Durand-Ruel, acting on behalf of the Art Institute, with funds provided by Adolphus C. Bartlett, 1900.
items.description_zh: 芝加哥艺术博物馆收藏的于贝尔·罗贝尔的四幅大型画作是由富有的金融家让-约瑟夫·拉博德侯爵委托创作的，用于装饰他在法国梅雷维尔城堡的沙龙。安装在房间的四面，它们会让人感觉墙壁消失了，展现出古典建筑的幻想，并点缀着日常生活场景。罗贝尔作为建筑废墟画家备受追捧，这一题材迎合了18世纪末对古物的品味。他通过十多年的意大利旅行和学习培养了对古物的知识。
items.cardline_zh: 残柱间，时光如藤蔓缠绕古典幻梦。
items.cardline: Broken columns, time vines through classical dream.

items.title: Near the Lake
items.provenance: Durand-Ruel, Paris, Sept. 24, 1886 [per Durand-Ruel, Paris, stock book for 1884–90 (no. 1340, Au bord du lac), as confirmed by Paul-Louis Durand-Ruel and Flavie Durand-Ruel, Durand-Ruel Archives, Oct. 5, 2010, curatorial object file]; acquired by Durand-Ruel, New York, about 1888 [per Durand-Ruel, New York, stock book for 1888–91 (no. 141, Au bord du lac), as confirmed by Paul-Louis Durand-Ruel and Flavie Durand-Ruel, Durand-Ruel Archives, Oct. 5, 2010, curatorial object file]; sold to Potter Palmer (d. 1902), Chicago, Mar. 2, 1892, for $900. [per Durand-Ruel, New York, stock book for 1888–93 (no. 197, Au bord du lac), as confirmed by Paul-Louis Durand-Ruel and Flavie Durand-Ruel, Durand-Ruel Archives, Oct. 5, 2010, curatorial object file]; by decent to the Palmer family; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1922.
items.cardline_zh: 湖光潋滟处，午后停在每一片树叶。
items.cardline: Lakelight shimmers, afternoon rests on every leaf.

items.title: The Policeman
items.description: Soon after Joan Miró moved to Paris from his native Barcelona in 1920, he met a group of avant-garde painters and writers who advocated merging the everyday rational world with that of dreams and the unconscious in order to produce an absolute reality, or surreality. To release images of this higher realm, the Surrealists embraced automatism, a spontaneous working method much like free association. Miró experimented with automatism: “Even a few casual wipes in cleaning my brush,” he said, “may suggest the beginning of a picture.” Between 1925 and 1927, his experiments unleashed a revolutionary series of works called the “dream paintings,” which straddle abstraction and representation in freely moving, calligraphic compositions. In The Policeman, a large canvas from this group, two biomorphic shapes spring to life as a policeman and a horse, their forms defined by thinly applied white paint against a neutral ocher ground. The form on the left has sprouted five buds that act as fingers, and both forms extrude curves that suggest torsos or mouths. With sketch-like dots and squiggles added to their heads to make eyes and a mustache, Miró’s shapes come to life in a liquid space as animated equivalents of a policeman and his horse.
items.provenance: Jacques Viot (Galerie Pierre), Paris, acquired directly from artist, late October–early November 1925 [Lanchner 1993]. René Gaffé, Brussels, by June 1928–at least 1962 [Lanchner 1993]; sold  to Norman Granz, Galerie Verve, Breganzona, Switzerland, around 1962–to at least April 1963 [Lanchner 1993]; sold to Richard Feigen Gallery, Chicago, April 1963 [letter April 12, 2001 from Richard Feigen and Co.]; sold to Claire Zeisler, Chicago, 27 April 1963–99 [letter listed above]; bequeathed to the Art Institite, 1999.
items.description_zh: 1920年，胡安·米罗从家乡巴塞罗那搬到巴黎后不久，他遇到了一群前卫画家和作家，他们主张将日常理性世界与梦境和无意识融合，以产生绝对现实，即超现实。为了释放这一更高领域的图像，超现实主义者接受了自动主义，一种类似于自由联想的自发工作方法。米罗尝试了自动主义：“甚至清理画笔时的偶然擦拭，”他说，“都可能暗示一幅画的开始。”在1925年至1927年间，他的实验释放了一系列革命性的作品，称为“梦境绘画”，它们在自由流动的书法式构图中介于抽象与具象之间。在这组作品中的一幅大画布《警察》中，两个生物形态的形状活跃起来，成为一匹马和一个警察，它们的形态由薄涂的白色颜料在中性赭色背景上定义。左侧的形状长出了五个芽作为手指，两个形状都伸出曲线暗示躯干或嘴巴。通过添加类似草图的点和涂鸦作为眼睛和胡子，米罗的形状在液体空间中活跃起来，成为警察和他的马的生动对应物。
items.cardline_zh: 自动的线条，窥见潜意识的街头哨兵。
items.cardline: Automatic lines glimpse the subconscious street guard.

items.title: Nocturne: Blue and Gold—Southampton Water
items.description: In the early 1870s James McNeill Whistler took a radical step toward abstraction with his Nocturnes series. In keeping with his art-for-art’s-sake creed, these works capture the stillness of evening while evoking a connection to music. Unlike his earlier marine paintings, the subject of this work—an inlet along the English Channel near Southampton—is obscured by the approaching night. Large shipping vessels appear as ghostly shapes, reduced to shadowy forms by the deepening twilight, while the only points of brightness come from the subtle reflections of lights and the fragmented orb of the moon. The setting thus serves primarily as a vehicle for Whistler’s interest in the harmonies of muted color amid darkness.
items.provenance: The artist, 1872; Alfred Chapman (1839–1917), London, June 1874 [letter from James McNeill Whistler to James Alfred Chapman, June 22, 1874, GUW 11251]. H. Wunderlich and Co., New York, by January 1900 [letter from James McNeill Whistler to John James Cowan, January 6, 1900, GUW 00739]; sold to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1900.
items.title_zh: 夜曲：蓝与金——南安普敦水道
items.description_zh: 1870年代初，詹姆斯·麦克尼尔·惠斯勒通过他的《夜曲》系列向抽象迈出了激进的一步。遵循他为艺术而艺术的信条，这些作品捕捉了夜晚的宁静，同时唤起与音乐的联系。与他早期的海洋画不同，这幅作品的主题——南安普敦附近英吉利海峡的一个小海湾——被临近的夜晚所遮蔽。大型船只以幽灵般的形状出现，被加深的暮色简化为模糊的形态，而唯一的光亮点来自灯光的微妙反射和月亮的碎片球体。因此，场景主要作为惠斯勒对黑暗中柔和色彩和谐的兴趣的载体。
items.cardline_zh: 蓝与金，夜色在水面奏响低音和弦。
items.cardline: Blue and gold, night plays bass chords on water.

items.title: The Place du Havre, Paris
items.description: After a period of experimentation with the Neo-Impressionist style developed by Georges Seurat, Camille Pissarro returned to the loose, multidirectional brushstrokes that he had used in his earlier Impressionist works. He also revisited an Impressionist subject that his colleagues had all but abandoned by the 1890s—the modern city. This bustling scene, alive with the noise and movement of traffic and pedestrians, was the view from his window at the Hôtel Garnier in Paris, where he stayed for a few weeks early in 1893. The building at the left edge of the canvas is the Gare Saint-Lazare.
items.provenance: The artist (d. 1903); sold to Durand-Ruel, Paris, March 17, 1893 [per Pissarro and Snollaerts 2005]; sold to Potter Palmer (d. 1902), Chicago, June 28, 1894, for $1,200 [per Durand-Ruel Archives, New York Stock no. 1168 and Paris Stock no. 2707, as Place du Hârve, as confirmed by Caroline Durand-Ruel Godfroy, Durand-Ruel Archives, to the Art Institute of Chicago, Dec. 13, 1994, curatorial object file]; by descent to the Potter family, Chicago; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1922.
items.description_zh: 在尝试了乔治·修拉发展的新印象派风格一段时间后，卡米耶·毕沙罗回到了他早期印象派作品中使用的松散、多方向的笔触。他还重新审视了一个到1890年代他的同行几乎已经放弃的印象派主题——现代城市。这个繁忙的场景，充满了交通和行人的噪音与运动，是他从巴黎加尼耶酒店窗户看到的景色，他在1893年初在那里住了几周。画布左边缘的建筑是圣拉扎尔火车站。
items.cardline_zh: 车马喧嚣，光点破碎成流动的河。
items.cardline: Traffic roars, light breaks into a flowing river.

items.title: The Petite Creuse River
items.description:
Claude Monet began this canvas—one of three of the Petite Creuse—in April 1889 but only returned to it later that spring, by which time the landscape had changed considerably. The oak tree, for example, was sprouting leaves, obscuring the view he had already established. Rather than rework or restart the canvas to depict the current season, Monet hired workers to defoliate the tree so that he could re-create its earlier appearance.
Monet spent three months in the remote Creuse valley in central France beginning in early March 1889 after visiting the region for a few days with art critic Gustave Geffroy in February. Despite bouts of poor health and bad weather, he returned to Giverny having painted 24 canvases. These constituted the artist’s first planned and rigorously defined series of paintings.
items.provenance: Potter Palmer, (d. 1902) Chicago [per Wildenstein 1996; Tucker 1989, p. 55 suggests that the painting was purchased by Berthe Palmer, not Potter Palmer]; by descent to the Potter Family, Chicago; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1922.
items.description_zh:
克劳德·莫奈于1889年4月开始创作这幅画布——三幅小克勒兹河作品之一——但直到那年春天晚些时候才重新拾起，此时风景已发生显著变化。例如，橡树已长出叶子，遮蔽了他原先确立的视野。莫奈没有重新绘制或修改画布以描绘当前季节，而是雇人摘掉树叶，以便重现其早先的面貌。
莫奈于1889年3月初开始，在法国中部偏远的克勒兹河谷度过了三个月，此前他在2月与艺术评论家古斯塔夫·热弗鲁瓦一起访问了该地区几天。尽管身体欠佳且天气恶劣，他仍带着24幅画作返回吉维尼。这些作品构成了艺术家首次有计划且严格定义的系列绘画。
items.cardline_zh: 春叶初绽，河水静流，时光在画中凝固
items.cardline: Spring leaves sprout, river flows still, time frozen in paint

items.title: Woman Reading
items.description:
During the late nineteenth century, Parisian cafés were the gathering places of artists and writers and were ideal locations for observing the urban scene. Many Impressionist paintings depict the Café Nouvelle-Athènes on the rue Pigalle, where two tables were reserved for Édouard Manet and his circle—a group that included the painters Edgar Degas, Claude Monet, Camille Pissarro, and Pierre-Auguste Renoir, and the writers Charles Baudelaire and Émile Zola.
At first glance, this fashionably dressed young woman appears to have been captured sitting at a favorite café: the marble tabletop, beer mug, and magazine attached to a wooden bar suggest such a setting, and her heavy clothing and kid gloves indicate that she is at an outdoor table and that the weather is cool. However, the floral background is actually one of Manet's paintings and the café a re-creation in his studio.
Woman Reading is one of the most Impressionist of Manet’s images; the quick, free brushstrokes and light colors are characteristic of his technique late in his career. Painted only a few years before his death, this work admirably captures a fleeting moment, the sense of modern life that Manet and his contemporaries sought to represent.
items.provenance: The artist; sold to Jean-Baptiste Faure (d. 1914), Paris, March 1882 [per Leenhoff, c. 1910, published online, fol. 81, https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/btv1b10548955r]; by descent to his son, Maurice Faure (1862-1915), Paris, probably through 1928 [per Tabarant 1931, p. 341; according to Bernheim-Jeune exhibition label on the stretcher from 1928, the painting was possibly still with the family]. Possibly Galerie Bernheim-Jeune, Paris, 1928 or 1929 [per Allan, Beeny, and Groom,  2019, p. 303]. Howard Young Galleries, New York, by 1929; sold to Mrs. Lewis Larned Coburn (née Annie Swan, d. 1932), Chicago, 1929 [per Art News 27, 40 (Sept. 14, 1929), p. 12]; bequeathed to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1933.
items.description_zh:
在19世纪末，巴黎的咖啡馆是艺术家和作家的聚集地，也是观察城市景象的理想场所。许多印象派画作描绘了皮加勒街上的新雅典咖啡馆，那里有两张桌子预留给爱德华·马奈及其圈子——包括画家埃德加·德加、克劳德·莫奈、卡米耶·毕沙罗和皮埃尔-奥古斯特·雷诺阿，以及作家夏尔·波德莱尔和埃米尔·左拉。
乍看之下，这位穿着时尚的年轻女子似乎被捕捉到坐在她最喜欢的咖啡馆里：大理石桌面、啤酒杯和固定在木杆上的杂志暗示了这样的场景，而她厚重的衣物和羊皮手套表明她坐在户外桌旁，天气凉爽。然而，花卉背景实际上是马奈的一幅画作，而咖啡馆则是他工作室中的再现。
《阅读中的女子》是马奈最具印象派风格的作品之一；快速、自由的笔触和明亮的色彩是他职业生涯后期技法的特点。这幅画作于他去世前几年，出色地捕捉了一个瞬间，体现了马奈及其同代人试图表现的现代生活感。
items.cardline_zh: 书页间，光影低语着巴黎的午后
items.cardline: Light whispers Paris afternoons between the pages

items.title: Beach at Cabasson (Baigne-Cul)
items.description: In 1891, as a result of his rheumatoid arthritis, Henri Edmond Cross moved to Cabasson, a hamlet on the Côte d’Azur in southeastern France. The move deeply affected his art: after settling on the Mediterranean coast, he embraced the divided brushwork (small dashes and dots of color) and tonal gradations of “scientific Impressionism.” This methodical technique was a departure from the Impressionists’ more intuitive quick strokes of color to capture fleeting atmospheric conditions. In this beach scene, Cross used this more systematic paint application to convey the color and heat of his adopted region.
items.provenance: Baron Eberhard Freiherr von Bodenhausen-Degener (1868 - 1918), Munich [according to Billeter 2001]; possibly by descent to his wife Dora Freifrau von Bodenhausen (1877-1969). Martin Fabiani, Paris [this and the following according to Gallery Fine Arts Associates' stock card, Otto Gerson Papers, Archives of American Art, copy in curatorial file]; sold to Gallery Fine Arts Associates (Otto Gerson), May 1951; sold to Mrs. Vincent Astor, Rhinebeck, New York, May 1951. Mr. and Mrs. Werner E. Josten, New York, by 1964 until at least 1968 [Compin 1964 and New York 1968]. Private Collection, Maryland [information given by R. L. Feigen]. Richard L. Feigen and Co., by 1982; sold to the Art Institute, 1983.
items.description_zh: 1891年，由于类风湿性关节炎，亨利·埃德蒙·克罗斯搬到了法国东南部蔚蓝海岸的小村庄卡巴松。这次搬迁深刻影响了他的艺术：在地中海沿岸定居后，他接受了“科学印象主义”的分割笔触（小色块和色点）和色调渐变。这种有条不紊的技法与印象派更直观的快速色彩笔触以捕捉短暂大气条件的方式不同。在这幅海滩场景中，克罗斯使用了这种更系统的颜料涂抹方式来传达他所居住地区的色彩和热度。
items.cardline_zh: 碎光与海风，在笔触间轻舞
items.cardline: Broken light and sea breeze dance in brushstrokes

items.title: Alfred Sisley
items.provenance: The artist (d. 1919); sold to Eugène Murer, Paris, by Apr. 1883 [as part of a lot of eleven paintings sold for 500 francs per Bailey 1997]; deposited with Durand-Ruel, Paris, 1883 [this and the following per Durand-Ruel, Paris, deposit book for 1879–84 (no. 3913, as Portrait de Sisley), as confirmed by Paul-Louis Durand-Ruel and Flavie Durand-Ruel, Oct. 5, 2010, curatorial object file]; returned to Eugène Murer, Paris, 1883; possibly sold by Eugène Murer, Paris, to Durand-Ruel, Paris, 1896 [according to Daulte 1971; Bailey 1997 states that the painting was sold to Durand-Ruel “at the time of the dispersal of his [Murer’s] collection,” although the Durand-Ruel Archives claim that the statement is false, as they have no record of ownership at the time, Paul-Louis Durand-Ruel and Flavie Durand-Ruel, Durand-Ruel Archives, to the Art Institute of Chicago, Oct. 5, 2010, curatorial object file]. Ivan Shchukin, Paris, by Mar. 1899 [Bailey 1997 states that Durand-Ruel sold the painting to the Russian collector Ivan Shchukin; the Durand-Ruel Archives disagree: “faux, pas vendu par Durand-Ruel Paris à Chtchoukine [sic].” See correspondence from Claire Durand-Ruel Snollaerts to the Art Institute of Chicago, Mar. 29, 2010, curatorial object file. See also Daulte 1971; Leclercq 1899 illustrates the painting and indicates that it is owned by Shchukin]; sold Shchukin Sale, Hôtel Drouot, Paris, March 24, 1900 to Dr. George Viau, Paris, for 6,100 francs [per Bailey 1997, although the Durand-Ruel Archives disagree: “faux, pas vendu par Durand-Ruel Paris à Chtchoukine [sic].” See correspondence from Claire Durand-Ruel Snollaerts to the Art Institute of Chicago, Mar. 29, 2010, curatorial object file. See also Daulte 1971; Leclercq 1899 illustrates the painting and indicates that it is owned by Shchukin]; Dr. George Viau, Paris, until at least June 20, 1912 [per Bailey 1997, it was exhibited by Durand-Ruel in Portraits par Renoir, June 5–20, 1912 (although the catalogue does not indicate this). See an archived verso label from the Collection George Viau, which includes a notation “Expo. Durand-Ru[el] 191[. . .]”; conservation file, Art Institute of Chicago]. Herman Beilbuth, by 1921 [per Ernst Jonas Bencard, Assistant Keeper, Ordrupgaardsamlingen, to the Art Institute of Chicago, Dec. 9, 1998, curatorial object file, Art Institute of Chicago]. Howard Young, New York [Daulte 1971]. Mrs. Lewis Larned (Annie Swan) Coburn (d. 1932), Chicago, by 1929 [Meier-Graefe 1929]; bequeathed to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1933.
items.cardline_zh: 目光里的温柔，是友谊的底色
items.cardline: Tender gaze paints the ground of friendship

items.title: Brushstroke with Spatter
items.description:
Roy Lichtenstein rose to prominence at the beginning of the 1960s and became one of the best-known practitioners of Pop Art. His brightly painted canvases depicting everyday objects, as well as scenes of war and romance culled from the pages of contemporary comics, were rendered in stylized black outlines and hand-painted Ben Day dots that mimicked the commercial printing process. Thus he seemed to elevate what was often seen as low art—both its quotidian, affective subject matter and its visual terms—into high art. This was never simply a straightforward process of reversal, however, and by mid-decade Lichtenstein began to focus on subjects that reflected his lifelong interest in art historical themes.
Brushstroke with Spatter, from the Brushstroke series (1965–67), is a wry commentary on the bravura of midcentury Abstract Expressionism. Here Lichtenstein combines the gestural sweep of Willem de Kooning’s brushwork with the rhythmic drips typical of Jackson Pollock. He isolates and magnifies his stroke and spatter against a ground of Ben Day dots, reproducing the conventionally painterly elements as flatly as possible with no trace of emotion or texture. This deadpan rendering is not purely comic, but also establishes a tension key to the work’s success as an abstract painting in its own right, with grand scalar play and dramatic cropping, intensity of color, and the optical pulse of the dotted ground.
items.description_zh:
罗伊·利希滕斯坦在20世纪60年代初崭露头角，成为波普艺术最著名的实践者之一。他色彩鲜艳的画布描绘日常物品，以及从当代漫画页面中提取的战争和浪漫场景，采用风格化的黑色轮廓和手绘的本戴点，模仿商业印刷过程。因此，他似乎将通常被视为低俗艺术的东西——无论是其平凡、感性的主题还是视觉语言——提升为高雅艺术。然而，这从来不是一个简单的逆转过程，到60年代中期，利希滕斯坦开始关注反映他对艺术史主题终生兴趣的题材。
《带飞溅的笔触》来自《笔触》系列（1965-67），是对世纪中叶抽象表现主义虚张声势的讽刺评论。在这里，利希滕斯坦将威廉·德·库宁笔触的姿势性挥洒与杰克逊·波洛克典型的节奏性滴洒相结合。他在本戴点的背景上隔离并放大了他的笔触和飞溅，尽可能平坦地再现了传统绘画元素，不带任何情感或质感。这种面无表情的渲染并非纯粹滑稽，而是建立了一种张力，这对作品作为抽象绘画本身的成功至关重要，具有宏大的尺度游戏和戏剧性的裁剪、强烈的色彩以及点状背景的光学脉动。
items.cardline_zh: 一笔狂放，溅出印刷时代的呼吸
items.cardline: One wild stroke splatters the breath of print age

items.title: Amédée-David, the Comte de Pastoret
items.description:
This portrait of Amédée-David, the Comte de Pastoret, is one of several artworks he commissioned from his friend Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres. Pastoret likely played a role in Ingres’s admission to the Académie des Beaux-Arts, and here he is seen wearing the embroidered black uniform reflective of his role as auditor for the Council of State. The shimmering Legion of Honor medal hung high around his neck with a red ribbon and the exquisitely detailed golden sword hilt alleviate the somber darkness of his costume. Pastoret stands outlined against forest-green silk wallpaper and a swag of matching drapery, with one hand resting on his hip and the other thumb tucked casually into his jacket. With confidence and swagger—and a less-than-subtle air of disinterest—Pastoret stares directly at the viewer with the grandeur typical of Ingres’s aristocratic portraits.
This painting stayed with Pastoret’s family until the death of his daughter in 1890, and in 1897 it was purchased by the famed Impressionist painter Edgar Degas. Ingres was a consistent source of artistic inspiration for Degas, himself a portraitist who owned some twenty paintings and over eighty drawings by the older artist. Degas kept this likeness of Pastoret in his art collection until his own death in 1917.
items.title_zh: 阿梅代-大卫，帕斯托雷伯爵
items.description_zh:
这幅阿梅代-大卫，帕斯托雷伯爵的肖像是他委托朋友让-奥古斯特-多米尼克·安格尔创作的多件艺术品之一。帕斯托雷可能在安格尔入选法兰西美术院中发挥了作用，这里他穿着绣花黑色制服，反映了他作为国务委员会审计员的角色。闪闪发光的荣誉军团勋章用红丝带高高挂在脖子上，以及精美细致的金色剑柄，缓解了他服装的阴沉黑暗。帕斯托雷站在森林绿色丝绸壁纸和相配的帷幔前，一只手放在臀部，另一只拇指随意地塞进夹克。带着自信和傲慢——以及一种不太微妙的冷漠气息——帕斯托雷直接凝视着观众，具有安格尔贵族肖像典型的宏伟气派。
这幅画一直留在帕斯托雷家族，直到他女儿于1890年去世，1897年被著名印象派画家埃德加·德加购得。安格尔一直是德加艺术灵感的源泉，德加本人也是一位肖像画家，拥有这位年长艺术家的约二十幅画作和八十多幅素描。德加将这幅帕斯托雷肖像保存在他的艺术收藏中，直到1917年去世。
items.cardline_zh: 勋章微光，衬出权柄下的沉思
items.cardline: Medal's gleam frames thought beneath power

items.title: View of Cotopaxi
items.description: View of Cotopaxi brings together in visual form scientific, religious, political, and cultural ideas in the mid-19th century. Inspired by German naturalist Alexander von Humboldt’s concept of ecological interconnectedness, Frederic Edwin Church traveled to South America to meticulously study the tropical landscape. The artist produced at least ten finished paintings of the Ecuadorian volcano, this one completed in his studio just before his second visit to the region. For Church and other Christian viewers, nature, with all its creative and destructive wonders, was evidence of divine power. The painting likewise reflected an imperialist vision, as US government officials eyed Latin America as a site for territorial expansion and conquest.
items.provenance: Walter Wright, Chicago, from 1857 to 1876; by descent to Anne E. Webster, from 1876 to 1894; by descent to Lewis Dana Webster, from 1894 to 1908; by descent to Ida Hamlin Webster, from 1908 to 1912; by descent to Mary Jennette Hamlin, from 1912 to 1919; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1919.
items.description_zh: 《科托帕希火山景观》以视觉形式汇集了19世纪中叶的科学、宗教、政治和文化观念。受德国博物学家亚历山大·冯·洪堡生态互联概念的启发，弗雷德里克·埃德温·丘奇前往南美洲仔细研究热带景观。艺术家创作了至少十幅厄瓜多尔火山的成品画作，这幅是在他第二次访问该地区之前在他的工作室完成的。对于丘奇和其他基督徒观众来说，自然及其所有创造性和破坏性的奇迹，是神圣力量的证据。这幅画也反映了帝国主义愿景，因为美国政府官员将拉丁美洲视为领土扩张和征服的地点。
items.cardline_zh: 火山静立，云霞吞吐天地的呼吸
items.cardline: Volcano still, clouds breathe the earth's rhythm

items.title: Still Life
items.description: Around 1600, as the Dutch economy boomed, large-scale still lifes depicting luxury goods emerged as a genre that appealed to a sophisticated clientele. Pieter Claesz. was a leading painter of a type of still life often described as a “banquet piece,” which featured sumptuous foods and opulent serving vessels, typically strewn across an elaborately dressed tabletop. These costly goods—like the lemon, olives, sweetmeats, and lace-edged damask tablecloth in this painting—were sometimes depicted in a state of decay to suggest the transitory nature of coveted earthly things. The well-preserved items here instead celebrate wealth and its lavish display.
items.provenance: Probably Cassirer, Amsterdam [the mounts of photos in both the Rijksbureau voor Kunsthistorische Documentatie, The Hague, and the Witt Library, London, are marked Cassirer, Amsterdam, 1939, though the picture was at the Art Institute from late 1935; the Witt photo is also annotated “Worcester, Mass.”; see also Vroom 1945 and Bergstrom 1956].  M. Knoedler & Co., New York by 1935 [receipt dated September 9, 1935 in Registrar’s office; the number A 1559 printed on a label on the back may be the Knoedler’s stock number]; sold to the Art Institute, 1935.
items.description_zh: 大约1600年，随着荷兰经济繁荣，描绘奢侈品的大型静物画作为一种吸引精致客户的流派出现。彼得·克拉斯是常被描述为“宴会作品”的静物画的主要画家，这些作品以丰盛的食物和华丽的餐具为特色，通常散落在精心布置的桌面上。这些昂贵的物品——如柠檬、橄榄、蜜饯和蕾丝边锦缎桌布——有时被描绘成腐烂状态，以暗示珍贵世俗事物的短暂性。这里保存完好的物品反而颂扬财富及其奢华展示。
items.cardline_zh: 柠檬与银器，静默中藏着奢华的箴言
items.cardline: Lemon and silver hold silent luxury's truth

items.title: Gasthof zur Muldentalsperre
items.description: Peter Doig creates dreamlike, psychologically charged landscapes that display a peculiar blend of abstraction and representation. Combining a hallucinatory palette with expressionist brushwork, his paintings have a mysterious, often melancholic air. Doig works exclusively from found images, drawing inspiration from film stills, newspaper clippings, personal photo albums, record album covers, and the work of earlier artists such as Edvard Munch and Ernst Ludwig Kirchner. The image of the two men in Gasthof zur Muldentalsperre derives from a photograph taken when the artist was working as a dresser at the London Coliseum. One night, after a production of Igor Stravinsky’s burlesque ballet Petrouchka (1911), Doig and a friend donned costumes from the performance and comically posed for a snapshot. The artist initially used the photo as the basis for a figure study; he then superimposed the two figures on a landscape he borrowed from an antique postcard, which depicts the vista from an old German tavern, Gasthof zur Muldentalsperre. In Doig’s version, the view has undergone strange chromatic shifts, resulting in a fantastical, nocturnal scene that resembles a theatrical backdrop. The men at the gate are an otherworldly presence; the winding path behind them seems to lead to another realm beyond the edge of the painting.
items.description_zh: 彼得·多伊格创作了梦幻般、充满心理张力的风景画，展现出抽象与具象的奇特融合。他将幻觉般的调色板与表现主义笔触相结合，使他的画作带有神秘、常常忧郁的气氛。多伊格完全从现成图像中创作，从电影剧照、报纸剪报、个人相册、唱片封面以及爱德华·蒙克和恩斯特·路德维希·基希纳等早期艺术家的作品中汲取灵感。《穆尔登塔尔水库客栈》中两个男人的形象来源于一张照片，当时艺术家在伦敦竞技场剧院担任服装师。一天晚上，在伊戈尔·斯特拉文斯基的滑稽芭蕾《彼得鲁什卡》（1911年）演出后，多伊格和一个朋友穿上表演服装，滑稽地摆姿势拍了一张快照。艺术家最初用这张照片作为人物研究的基础；然后他将两个人物叠加在一个他从古董明信片中借用的风景上，该明信片描绘了从一家德国老客栈穆尔登塔尔水库客栈看到的景色。在多伊格的版本中，景色经历了奇怪的色彩变化，产生了一个奇幻的夜景，类似于舞台背景。门口的男人是一种超自然的存在；他们身后蜿蜒的小路似乎通向画布边缘之外的另一个领域。
items.cardline_zh: 旅店沉入梦境，色彩摇曳成记忆的波纹
items.cardline: Inn sinks into dream, colors ripple memory

items.title: Approaching Storm
items.description:
This painting by Eugène Boudin, a Norman painter and Claude Monet’s teacher, exemplifies the artist’s signature style and subject matter in the 1860s. Monet would later follow Boudin’s practice of painting in the open air, the technique that gave this image of a beach for middle-class vacationers its vivid spontaneity and atmospheric light. This practice would be particularly important for Monet and his contemporaries, who came to be known as the Impressionists.
Eugène-Louis Boudin painted many beach scenes at the fashionable tourist spots of Trouville and Deauville in Normandy. In them we see the life of the Paris boulevards translated to the seashore. Bathing attire being still relatively rare, people wore the same clothes at the beach as they did in the city and made the same round of promenades, visits, and dinners. The huts on wheels are portable changing machines that were pulled into the water so that bathers could change without fear of exposure.
items.provenance: Mrs. Meredith Hare (Elizabeth Sage Goodwin, later Elizabeth Sage Hare, died c. 1949) [according to AIC Bulletin 1939 she had acquired it at a New York Sale].  Maynard Walker Gallery, New York by 1938; purchased by the Art Institute, 1938 using Coburn funds [see minutes from meeting of the Committee on Painting and Sculpture, AIC, October 5, 1938].
items.description_zh:
这幅画由诺曼底画家、克劳德·莫奈的老师欧仁·布丹创作，体现了艺术家在1860年代的标志性风格和主题。莫奈后来追随布丹在户外绘画的实践，这种技法赋予了这幅中产阶级度假者海滩图像生动的自发性和大气光线。这种实践对莫奈及其后来被称为印象派的同代人尤为重要。
欧仁-路易·布丹在诺曼底的特鲁维尔和多维尔等时尚旅游景点绘制了许多海滩场景。在这些画中，我们看到巴黎林荫大道的生活转移到了海边。由于泳装仍然相对罕见，人们在海滩上穿着与城市相同的衣服，并进行同样的散步、拜访和晚餐活动。带轮子的小屋是可移动的更衣机，被拉入水中，以便游泳者可以更换衣服而不必担心暴露。
items.cardline_zh: 风暴将至，天空已开始低语
items.cardline: Storm approaches, sky already whispers

items.title: Mao
items.medium: Acrylic, silkscreen ink, and pencil on linen
items.description: The most influential of Pop artists, Andy Warhol cast a cool, ironic light on the pervasiveness of commercial culture and contemporary celebrity worship. Early in his career, he began to utilize the silk-screen process to transfer photographed images to canvas, creating multiple portraits of celebrities including Marilyn Monroe, Elvis Presley, and Jacqueline Kennedy, as well as duplicated images of mass-produced products such as Campbell’s soup cans and Coca-Cola bottles. In this example from his Mao series, Warhol melded his signature style with the scale of totalitarian propaganda to address the cult of personality surrounding the Chinese ruler Mao Zedong (1893–1976). Nearly fifteen feet tall, this towering work mimics the representations of the political figure that were ubiquitously displayed throughout China. Warhol’s looming portrait impresses us with the duality of its realistic qualities and its plastic artificiality. In contrast to the photographic nature of the image, garish colors are applied to Mao’s face like makeup. The gestural handling of color in the portrait shows Warhol at his most painterly.
items.provenance: Sold by the artist through Leo Castelli Gallery, New York, to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1974.
items.description_zh: 作为最具影响力的波普艺术家，安迪·沃霍尔对商业文化的普遍性和当代名人崇拜投以冷静、讽刺的目光。在他职业生涯早期，他开始使用丝网印刷工艺将摄影图像转移到画布上，创作了玛丽莲·梦露、埃尔维斯·普雷斯利和杰奎琳·肯尼迪等多位名人的多重肖像，以及坎贝尔汤罐和可口可乐瓶等大规模生产产品的复制图像。在这幅来自他的毛泽东系列的作品中，沃霍尔将他的标志性风格与极权主义宣传的规模相结合，以处理围绕中国统治者毛泽东（1893-1976）的个人崇拜。这幅近十五英尺高的巨作模仿了在中国各地普遍展示的政治人物形象。沃霍尔高耸的肖像以其现实品质和塑料人工性的二元性给我们留下深刻印象。与图像的摄影性质形成对比，鲜艳的色彩像化妆品一样涂在毛泽东的脸上。肖像中色彩的手势处理显示了沃霍尔最具绘画性的时刻。
items.cardline_zh: 面孔重复，偶像在复制的洪流中消融
items.cardline: Face repeats, icon dissolves in copy's flood

items.title: The Fountains
items.description: The painting of Classical ruins had reached the zenith of its popularity when Hubert Robert, the leading French practitioner of this specialty, was commissioned in 1787 to paint a suite of four canvases for a wealthy financier’s château at Méréville, near Paris. The Fountains and its companion pieces were set into the paneled walls of a salon in the château, creating an alternate space that played off of the elegant, Neoclassical decor of the room. Robert has studied in Rome from 1754 to 1765 and there had gleaned his artistic vocabulary. Like the other three large paintings from the group painted for Méréville, all in the Art Institute, The Fountains exploits Robert’s typical vocabulary of fictive niches, arches, coffered vaults, colonnades, majestic stairwells, and Roman statuary to create a fantasy of expansive space. The four paintings are inhabited by tiny figures in the foreground; these serve only to set the scale and animate the scene, for the ruins themselves are the true subject of the pictures. In his use of ruins, Robert embodied the notion of the relationship of mankind and the built environment to nature that was expressed by the French philosopher and encyclopedist Denis Diderot: “Everything vanishes, everything dies, only time endures.”
items.provenance: Commissioned with its pendants (1900.382, 1900.383, 1900.384) by Jean Joseph, marquis de Laborde (died 1794), in 1787 for the Château de Méréville (near Etampes); the château was sold by Mme de Laborde, 1819 [see Simone de Lassus, “Quelques Détails inédits sur Méréville,” Bulletin de la Société de l’histoire de l’art français, année 1976 (1978), p. 286 n. 1]; the château was owned successively by: M. Ters and Mme d’Espagnat (sold 1824); comte de Saint-Roman (sold 1866); duc de Sessa (sold 1868); M. and Mme Beleys (sold 1869); la Société Cail (sold 1874); M. Heddle (sold 1889); Adam Natanson (sold 1890); M. Hériot (sold 1896); Prudent Carpentier (sold 1897, at which time the contents of the house were dispersed and the paintings probably sold) [See Simone de Lassus 1978, cited above; a letter from Bernard Binvel to Susan Wise dated May 5, 1987 states, perhaps erroneously, that Hériot sold the paintings in July of 1896]. M. L. François; sold Galerie George Petit, Paris, June 13, 1900, no. 3, to Durand-Ruel, acting on behalf of the Art Institute, with funds provided by William G. Hibbard, 1900.
items.description_zh: 当法国古典废墟绘画的主要实践者于贝尔·罗贝尔于1787年受委托为巴黎附近梅雷维尔的一位富有金融家的城堡绘制一套四幅画布时，古典废墟绘画的流行已达到顶峰。《喷泉》及其配套作品被嵌入城堡沙龙镶板墙壁中，创造了一个与房间优雅的新古典主义装饰相呼应的替代空间。罗贝尔于1754年至1765年在罗马学习，并在那里汲取了他的艺术词汇。像为梅雷维尔绘制的该组其他三幅大型画作（均在芝加哥艺术博物馆）一样，《喷泉》利用了罗贝尔典型的虚构壁龛、拱门、拱形天花板、柱廊、宏伟楼梯和罗马雕像词汇，创造了一个广阔空间的幻想。四幅画的前景中都有微小的人物；这些人物仅用于设定比例和活跃场景，因为废墟本身才是画作真正的主题。在使用废墟时，罗贝尔体现了法国哲学家和百科全书家德尼·狄德罗所表达的人与建筑环境与自然关系的概念：“一切消失，一切死亡，只有时间永存。”
items.cardline_zh: 喷泉无声，废墟间流淌着古典的余韵
items.cardline: Fountain silent, ruins echo classical grace

items.title: Bullfight
items.description: In the fall of 1865, Édouard Manet traveled to Spain for about ten days. This brief trip had a profound impact on his art of this period. In a letter to poet Charles Baudelaire, he described a bullfight he attended in Madrid as “one of the finest, most curious, and most terrifying sights to be seen.” The quick sketches he made as he watched the fights informed several later canvases, including this one. Here, he underscored the tension of the moment—the crowd in the background blends together in a blur while the bullfighter and bull stand off in sharper focus.
items.description_zh: 1865年秋天，爱德华·马奈前往西班牙旅行了大约十天。这次短暂的旅行对他这一时期的艺术产生了深远影响。在给诗人夏尔·波德莱尔的一封信中，他将在马德里观看的一场斗牛描述为“所见最精彩、最奇特、最恐怖的景象之一”。他在观看斗牛时快速绘制的速写为后来的几幅画布提供了灵感，包括这幅。在这里，他强调了那一刻的紧张——背景中的人群模糊地融合在一起，而斗牛士和公牛则以更清晰的焦点对峙。
items.cardline_zh: 牛角与尘土，凝固了那一声狂热的呐喊
items.cardline: Horn and dust freeze that fervent cry

items.title: The Feast in the House of Simon
items.artist:
El Greco (Doménikos Theotokópoulos; Greek, active in Spain, 1541–1614)
Workshop of El Greco (Doménikos Theotokópoulos; Greek, active in Spain, 1541–1614)
items.description: Although this painting features the bold highlights, elongated figures, and vibrant colors characteristic of El Greco’s late career, the artist enlisted his workshop to execute some parts of the scene according to his original design. The heads of the figures reflect the work of different artists, probably including the artist’s son, Jorge Manuel, a leader of the workshop in its later years. The fantastical architectural background is unlike any other by El Greco and may have been conceived by an assistant.
items.provenance: Possibly in the artist’s possession at the time of his death, 1614 [three versions of this subject were recorded in the 1621 inventory of all the goods owned by El Greco’s son, Jorge Manuel Theotocopoulos, who was his father’s sole heir; however, no work of this subject appears in El Greco’s own estate inventory; see San Román 1927]; possibly by descent to his son, Jorge Manuel Theotocopoulos (d. 1631), Toledo [the 1621 inventory of Jorge Manuel’s possessions, transcribed in San Román 1927, includes entries for three versions of this subject, listed as items 123–25].. Probably Guinea, Bilbao.4 Plasencia, Bilbao, before 1908 [according to Cossío 1908; this information cannot be verified, however]. Alexandre Berthier, Prince of Wagram, Paris, by 1908 [Cossio 1908 and Mayer 1926]; Galerie H. O. Miethke, Vienna, by 1911 Mayer 1911, p. 84]; sold to Durand-Ruel, New York, by 1914 [Kehrer 1914, p. 93; Mayer 1914]. Joseph Winterbotham, Jr., Burlington, Vt., Feb. 7, 1929 [Art News 1929];  on loan to the Art Institute in 1930 [Bulletin of the Art Institute of Chicago 1930]; acquired by the Art Institute as a purchase of the Winterbotham Fund, 1949.
items.description_zh: 尽管这幅画具有埃尔·格列柯晚期职业生涯特征的大胆高光、拉长人物和鲜艳色彩，但艺术家委托他的工作室根据他的原始设计执行了场景的某些部分。人物的头部反映了不同艺术家的作品，可能包括艺术家的儿子豪尔赫·曼努埃尔，他是工作室后期的领导者。奇幻的建筑背景与埃尔·格列柯的任何其他作品都不同，可能由一位助手构思。
items.cardline_zh: 宴席之上，圣与凡在光影中交错
items.cardline: At feast, sacred and mortal blend in light

items.title: Bathers by a River
items.description:
Henri Matisse considered Bathers by a River to be one of the five most “pivotal” works of his career, and with good reason: it facilitated the evolution of the artist’s style over the course of nearly a decade. Originally, the work was related to a 1909 commission by the Russian collector Sergei Shchukin, who wanted two large canvases to decorate the staircase of his Moscow home. Matisse proposed three pastoral images, though Shchukin decided to purchase only two works, Dance II and Music (both State Hermitage Museum, Saint Petersburg).
Four years later, Matisse returned to this canvas, the rejected third image, altering the idyllic scene and changing the pastel palette to reflect his new interest in Cubism. He reordered the composition, making the figures more columnar, with faceless, ovoid heads. Over the next years, Matisse transformed the background into four vertical bands and turned the formerly blue river into a thick black vertical band. With its restricted palette and severely abstracted forms, Bathers by a River is far removed from Dance II and Music, which convey a graceful lyricism. The sobriety and hint of danger in Bathers by a River may in part reflect the artist’s concerns during the terrible, war-torn period during which he completed it.
items.provenance: The artist; sold to Paul Guillaume (1891–1934), Paris, Sept. 14, 1926 [Chicago 2010]; by descent to his wife, Mme. Paul Guillaume (a.k.a. Domenica, née Juliette Léonie Lacaze; remarried as Mme. Jean Walter, 1898–1977), Paris, 1934; sold to Henry Pearlman (1895–1974), New York, summer 1951 [letter from Rose Pearlman, Dec. 4, 1975; copy in curatorial file]; acquired through exchange by the Art Institute of Chicago, Apr. 2, 1953.
items.description_zh:
亨利·马蒂斯认为《河畔浴者》是他职业生涯中五件最“关键”的作品之一，这有充分理由：它促进了艺术家风格近十年的演变。最初，这件作品与1909年俄罗斯收藏家谢尔盖·舒金的委托有关，他想要两幅大画布装饰他莫斯科家中的楼梯。马蒂斯提出了三幅田园图像，但舒金决定只购买两幅作品，《舞蹈 II》和《音乐》（均藏于圣彼得堡国立艾尔米塔什博物馆）。
四年后，马蒂斯重新拾起这幅被拒绝的第三幅图像，改变了田园场景，并将粉彩调色板改为反映他对立体主义的新兴趣。他重新安排了构图，使人物更加柱状，具有无脸的卵形头部。在接下来的几年里，马蒂斯将背景转变为四个垂直带，并将以前蓝色的河流变成一条粗黑的垂直带。凭借其受限的调色板和严重抽象的形式，《河畔浴者》与传达优雅抒情性的《舞蹈 II》和《音乐》相去甚远。《河畔浴者》中的严肃和危险暗示可能部分反映了艺术家在完成它期间可怕的战争时期的担忧。
items.cardline_zh: 河岸的形体，在简化中逼近永恒
items.cardline: Forms by river simplify toward eternity

items.title: Arlésiennes (Mistral)
items.description:
During the winter of 1888, Paul Gauguin spent a troubled two months with Vincent van Gogh in the southern French city of Arles. He painted the enigmatic Arlésiennes (Mistral) during this stay. The painters’ time together was intended to be the beginning of an artist’s colony, but their relationship grew increasingly tense, and Van Gogh’s mental health deteriorated.
Set directly across the street from the house they shared, the painting depicts four women somberly processing through a public garden. The space is tilted radically upward, and complex, three-dimensional forms are reduced to simple, flat shapes. For example, twin orange-yellow cones—probably protective hay coverings to shield plants from the frost—tower at the right like abstracted human figures. Wrapped in dark shawls, the two closest women cover their mouths against the frigid air. Their gestures are withdrawn and introspective, and their passage is seemingly blocked by a bright red fence and large green bush. Perhaps the composition’s oddest element is the appearance of a face within the bush. This face—an intentional inclusion by the artist—adds an uncanny, watchful presence to the scene. It exemplifies Gauguin’s exploration of what he believed to be the mysteries that suffuse everyday life.
items.provenance: Sold by the artist to Théo van Gogh for 300 Francs probably in the first half of 1889 [according to "carnet de l'artiste," 1888-1889, see Huyghe 1952, p. 223];  Émile Schuffenecker [according to Jeanne Schuffenecker, see Wildenstein 2001]; Thannhauser Galerie, Lucerne, c. 1923, stock no. 1167 [according to Wildenstein 2001]. James W. Barney, New York, c. 1929 [according to  New York 1929 exh. cat.].  De Hauke and Co., New York, 1930 [according to  Providence, R. I. 1930 exh. cat.].  Jacques Seligmann and Co., New York by 1931 [according to St. Louis 1931 exh. cat.; De Hauke and Seligmann may have owned the picture together since they were partners until about 1931]; sold to the Art Institute, October 26, 1934, using funds from the Mr. and Mrs. Lewis Larned Coburn Fund.
items.title_zh: 阿尔勒女人（密史脱拉风）
items.description_zh:
1888年冬天，保罗·高更与文森特·梵高在法国南部城市阿尔勒度过了不安的两个月。他在此期间绘制了神秘的《阿尔勒女人（密史脱拉风）》。两位画家在一起的时光本应是艺术家聚居地的开始，但他们的关系日益紧张，梵高的心理健康状况恶化。
这幅画描绘了四个女人阴沉地穿过一个公共花园，该花园位于他们合住房屋的街对面。空间被急剧向上倾斜，复杂的三维形式被简化为简单的平面形状。例如，双橙黄色锥体——可能是保护植物免受霜冻的干草覆盖物——像抽象的人物一样耸立在右侧。两个最近的女人裹着深色披肩，捂住嘴巴抵御寒冷空气。她们的手势退缩而内省，她们的通道似乎被一道鲜红的栅栏和一大片绿色灌木阻挡。也许构图中最奇怪的元素是灌木中出现的一张脸。这张脸——艺术家的有意包含——为场景增添了一种神秘、警觉的存在。它体现了高更对他认为弥漫在日常生活中的奥秘的探索。
items.cardline_zh: 风中的女子，裙摆拂过阿尔勒的萧瑟
items.cardline: Woman in wind, her gown brushes Arles' chill

items.title: Head of a Guillotined Man
items.description: Théodore Géricault painted this macabre image directly from life. The artist is known to have acquired corpses from his local morgue to study anatomy and the effects of decomposition. Surrounding himself with the stench of decay, he produced several paintings of decapitated heads and severed limbs. By depicting this graying and lifeless head upon on a blood-stained cloth laid over a wooden table, Géricault also referenced—perhaps ironically—the long history of still-life painting in Western art. The head probably belonged to a convicted criminal. At that time in France, executions were carried out by the guillotine, a bladed device that sliced through the necks of its victims.
items.provenance: The artist’s estate sale, Hôtel de Buillion, November 2-3, 1824 [possibly no. 17, “Dix études de diverses parties du corps humain” or handwritten addition to a copy of the catalogue in the British Museum, “trois têtes de guillotinés peints peu de temps après...270 (frs.)”; see Eitner 1959]. The painter Auguste Boulard (died 1897), Paris; his estate sale, Pairs, Hôtel Drouot, April 9–10, 1900, no. 130.  Édouard Napoléon César Edmond Mortier, duc de Trévise (died 1946), Paris, from at least 1924 until at least 1937 [lent by to Paris 1924 and Paris 1937]. Pierre Dubaut (died 1968) by 1952 [lent by him to London, 1952 and later exhibitions; Dubaut may have acquired it directly from the Duc de Trévise, since they were co-authors of Paris 1924 and Dubaut was the author of Paris 1937]; by descent to his niece Jacqueline Dubaut- Bellonte, Paris; purchased by the Art Institute through her gallery, Galerie du Fleuve, 1992.
items.description_zh: 泰奥多尔·热里科直接根据生活绘制了这幅令人毛骨悚然的图像。已知艺术家从当地太平间获取尸体以研究解剖学和腐烂效果。他置身于腐烂的恶臭中，创作了几幅断头和断肢的画作。通过描绘这个灰白无生命的头颅放在铺在木桌上的血迹斑斑的布上，热里科也——也许是讽刺地——参照了西方艺术中静物画的悠久历史。这个头颅可能属于一名被定罪的罪犯。当时在法国，处决是通过断头台执行的，这是一种用刀片切断受害者脖子的装置。
items.cardline_zh: 断首之上，死亡以真实之姿凝视
items.cardline: Severed head, death stares with brutal truth

items.title: Jupiter Rebuked by Venus
items.description: Abraham Janssens, Peter Paul Rubens's major competitor in Antwerp in the 1610s, produced monumental paintings of mythological and secular subjects. Influenced by his five-year stay in Rome, he injected his paintings with recognizable quotations from ancient sculpture and Italian Renaissance painting. This scene of Venus reprimanding the visibly annoyed Jupiter on Mount Olympus is a direct reference to a composition by the Italian artist Raphael on the ceiling of the Villa Farnesina in Rome. Janssens, however, enhanced the power and dynamism of the figures by emphasizing their musculature and working on a larger scale.
items.provenance: John Gage Predergast Vereker, 5th Viscount Gort (died 1902), Durham; offered for sale by his estate, Christie’s, London, 20 December 1902, lot 115, as Luca Giordano, bought in. Private collection, Ireland. Sold, Sotheby’s, London, 17 November 1982, no. 12, to Patrick Matthiesen, London; sold to the Art Institute, December 1986.
items.description_zh: 亚伯拉罕·扬森斯是1610年代安特卫普彼得·保罗·鲁本斯的主要竞争对手，创作了神话和世俗题材的巨幅画作。受他在罗马五年逗留的影响，他在画作中注入了可识别的古代雕塑和意大利文艺复兴绘画的引用。这幅维纳斯在奥林匹斯山上斥责明显恼怒的朱庇特的场景，直接参照了意大利艺术家拉斐尔在罗马法尔内西纳别墅天花板上的构图。然而，扬森斯通过强调人物的肌肉组织和在更大尺度上工作，增强了人物的力量和动感。
items.cardline_zh: 神祇的嗔怒，在云端凝固成永恒的戏剧
items.cardline: Gods' quarrel freezes into eternal drama on clouds

items.title: A Marine
items.description: George Inness’s rough, energetic brushstrokes evoke the raw beauty and potentially destructive power of the ocean in this dramatic seascape. He created it during a trip to Europe in the early 1870s. It was previously thought to depict Étretat, in Normandy, France, but scholars now believe that the image is of Porto d’Anzio, Italy. The sky is illuminated by patches of orange and white light, outlining the ships and distant town on the horizon. Careful study of the surface of the central rock in the foreground illustrates the artist’s impressive manipulation of paint: by employing shifts in texture and shading, he was able to emphasize distinctions between sun and shadow, wet and dry, and clean and algae-covered. Inness traveled to Europe to study landscape painting with the masters of the Barbizon School, who were known for a personal style that contrasted with the grandiose Hudson River School in America. The influence of this intimate approach is apparent in the painting of the rocks and spraying seawater.
items.provenance: Harry Reinhardt, New York, by 1913; Edward B. Butler, Chicago, 1913; given to the Art Institute of Chicago, 1913.
items.description_zh: 乔治·英尼斯粗犷、充满活力的笔触在这幅戏剧性的海景中唤起了海洋的原始美和潜在破坏力。他在1870年代初的欧洲旅行期间创作了这幅画。以前认为它描绘了法国诺曼底的埃特勒塔，但现在学者们认为图像是意大利安齐奥港。天空被橙色和白色的光斑照亮，勾勒出地平线上的船只和远处城镇。仔细研究前景中央岩石的表面，展示了艺术家对颜料的惊人操控：通过运用纹理和阴影的变化，他能够强调阳光与阴影、湿与干、清洁与藻类覆盖之间的区别。英尼斯前往欧洲与巴比松画派的大师学习风景画，该画派以个人风格著称，与美国宏大的哈德逊河画派形成对比。这种亲密方法的影响在岩石和飞溅海水的绘画中显而易见。
items.cardline_zh: 海天之间，笔触激荡着自然的野性
items.cardline: Between sea and sky, strokes stir nature's wild

items.title: Lozenge Composition with Yellow, Black, Blue, Red, and Gray
items.description: Piet Mondrian, a painter of the revolutionary international movement De Stijl (the Style), argued that "the straight line tells the truth." Why, then, we might wonder, would he choose to hang a painting off axis, where its edges imply dynamic diagonals? Among other motivations, rotating the canvas allowed Mondrian to reconsider a question he spent his career exploring, namely, the relationship between the contents of a painting and what contains them. In Lozenge Composition, the squared-off black lines imply enclosure, while a single line (above the blue area) extends to the slanted edge, suggesting extension beyond the canvas. This implication of what might lie beyond also prompted Mondrian to invoke the full expanse of the wall by hanging diagonal paintings well above eye level.
items.provenance: Consigned to Galerie de “L’Effort Moderne” (Léonce Rosenberg), Paris, 1921-22. Jakob von Domselaer and Maaike von Domselaer-Middelkoop, Bergen, the Netherlands, 1922(?)-1940/45. John Rädecker, Groet, the Netherlands, 1940/45-c. 1948. John L. Senior, Jr., New York, by 1949-1956 [letter from Sidney Janis Gallery in curatorial file]. Sidney Janis Gallery, New York, 1956. Edgar Kaufmann, Jr., New York, March 1, 1957; given to the Art Institute, 1957.
items.description_zh: 皮特·蒙德里安，革命性国际运动风格派的画家，主张“直线说出真相”。那么，我们可能会想，他为什么选择将画作偏离轴线悬挂，使其边缘暗示动态对角线？在其他动机中，旋转画布使蒙德里安能够重新思考他整个职业生涯中探索的一个问题，即画作内容与容纳它们的东西之间的关系。在《菱形构图》中，方形的黑色线条暗示封闭，而一条单独的线（蓝色区域上方）延伸到倾斜的边缘，暗示画布之外的延伸。这种对可能之外的含义也促使蒙德里安通过将对角线画作悬挂在远高于视线水平的位置来调用墙壁的全部范围。
items.cardline_zh: 色块方格，在旋转中找到了平衡的支点
items.cardline: Color blocks find balance in rotation's pivot
